title: Dematerialization Assessment Algorithm
description: Algorithm to assess the dematerialization potential of a product or service
code:
ALGORITHM AssessDematerializationPotential
INPUT: ProductType, CurrentMaterials, FunctionalValue
OUTPUT: DematerializationScore, Recommendation

BEGIN
 SET materialScore = CalculateMaterialIntensity(CurrentMaterials)
 SET functionalScore = MeasureFunctionalValue(FunctionalValue)
 SET virtualAlternative = CheckVirtualAlternatives(ProductType)
 
 IF virtualAlternative EXISTS THEN
 SET strongPotential = TRUE
 SET reductionEstimate = CalculateMaterialReduction(100%)
 RETURN "Strong Dematerialization: Replace with virtual alternative"
 ELSE
 SET lightWeightOptions = CheckLightweighting(ProductType)
 IF lightWeightOptions EXISTS THEN
 SET reductionEstimate = CalculateMaterialReduction(30-50%)
 RETURN "Weak Dematerialization: Implement lightweighting"
 ELSE
 SET circularOptions = CheckCircularEconomy(ProductType)
 IF circularOptions EXISTS THEN
 RETURN "Circular Economy: Implement reuse/recycle"
 END IF
 END IF
 END IF
END
explanation: This algorithm helps evaluate dematerialization opportunities by checking for virtual alternatives first (strong dematerialization), then lightweighting options (weak dematerialization), and finally circular economy approaches.

title: Life Cycle Assessment Calculator
description: Simplified LCA calculation for comparing physical vs dematerialized products
code:
ALGORITHM CompareLCA
INPUT: PhysicalProductData, VirtualProductData
OUTPUT: EnvironmentalImpactComparison

BEGIN
 SET categories = ["Energy", "Emissions", "Materials", "Waste"]
 
 FOR EACH category IN categories DO
 physical = PhysicalProductData[category]
 virtual = VirtualProductData[category]
 
 SET reduction = ((physical - virtual) / physical) * 100
 
 IF reduction > 0 THEN
 PRINT category + ": " + reduction + "% reduction with dematerialization"
 ELSE
 PRINT category + ": No reduction (possible increase)"
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 totalScore = CalculateTotalImpact(PhysicalProductData) - CalculateTotalImpact(VirtualProductData)
 RETURN totalScore
END
explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how LCA compares environmental impacts between physical and dematerialized alternatives across energy, emissions, materials, and waste categories.

complexity.time: O(n) where n = number of categories

title: Product Chain Material Flow Analysis
description: Algorithm to track and reduce material flow through product chain stages
code:
ALGORITHM MaterialFlowReduction
INPUT: ProductChain[5], InitialMaterialQuantity
OUTPUT: MaterialAtEachStage, ReductionPoints

BEGIN
 stages = ["Extraction", "Manufacturing", "Distribution", "Use", "Disposal"]
 materials[0] = InitialMaterialQuantity
 
 FOR i = 1 TO 4 DO
 wasteFactor = GetWasteFactor(stages[i])
 efficiencyFactor = GetEfficiencyFactor(stages[i])
 
 materials[i] = materials[i-1] * (1 - wasteFactor) * efficiencyFactor
 END FOR
 
 FOR EACH stage IN stages DO
 reduction = InitialMaterialQuantity - materials[index]
 IF reduction > (InitialMaterialQuantity * 0.2) THEN
 ADD stage TO ReductionPoints
 PRINT "High reduction opportunity at: " + stage
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN materials, ReductionPoints
END
explanation: This algorithm models material flow through each product chain stage and identifies high-impact points for dematerialization interventions.

complexity.time: O(n) where n = number of stages
